summary: Justin Brennan coaches Angelica through analyzing a 24-unit multifamily property in Miami worth $4.75M, focusing on proper underwriting methodology, expense estimation using ChatGPT for tax calculations, and creative seller financing structures. He demonstrates how to extract unit mix and rent data from rent rolls, project future rents based on market comparables, and structure a deal where sellers become lenders to avoid massive tax events while generating monthly income.
takeaways: Extract unit mix from rent rolls by asking 'what are your unit types' and count each type separately (e.g., 15 x 1BR @ $1,500 avg, 9 x 2BR @ $2,100 avg)

Use ChatGPT for property tax estimation: input property address + purchase price and ask for 'projected property taxes annually based on assessed value' - then adjust for 90% of purchase price if needed
For expense ratios on class-B/C properties aged 1950s in Florida: target 40-48% expense ratio to EGI (effective gross income)
Management fees should be 8% of gross potential rents for professional third-party management
Maintenance and repairs for older properties: budget at least $500-750/month for reasonable estimates
Contract services (landscaping, etc.) typically minimum $500/month - verify actual contracts with broker

Seller financing strategy: if sellers have no debt, offer $600K down payment + $28K/month for 5-year note at 6.25% interest, allowing them to avoid tax event, receive lump sum, get monthly income, and avoid property management
Use cap rate formula: NOI ÷ purchase price = cap rate; ensure cap rate > loan interest rate (e.g., 6.71% cap > 6.25% loan = positive spread)
Acquisition fee calculation: 2% of purchase price ($4.75M deal = $95K fee); as GP you can structure deal where acquisition fee covers your equity requirement

skill_candidates.change: Systematize rent roll extraction by unit type rather than entering all 24 units individually
skill_candidates.evidence: Justin teaches Angelica to ask 'what unit types exist' then count (6x 1BR, 8x 2BR, 10x 2BR) and input average rents by type instead of manual per-unit entry

skill_candidates.change: Use ChatGPT to automate property tax estimation instead of manual county website research
skill_candidates.evidence: Justin demonstrates inputting property address + purchase price into ChatGPT to generate tax projections, eliminating need to manually parse county assessment data

skill_candidates.change: Establish expense benchmarks by property class/age (40-48% EGI for older Florida multifamily) to validate broker-provided numbers
skill_candidates.evidence: Justin reviews Angelica's 76% expense ratio, identifies formula error, recalculates to 41% EGI which 'makes sense' for 1958 Miami property, teaching her to validate against benchmarks

skill_candidates.change: Ask targeted questions to identify seller financing opportunity before proposing structure
skill_candidates.evidence: Justin instructs Angelica to first ask broker: 'Do your parents have a loan?' then 'What's most important - daily management burden, income stream, or tax avoidance?' to uncover seller motivation before pitching seller financing solution

title: Weekly Coaching_06-26-25 | Justin Brennan
